figures.caption: Fig. 1: Rate savings vs. HEVC on the CLIC 2020 [17] dataset as a function of decoding complexity. Negative values mean fewer bits than HEVC at equal quality. Circle area denotes encoding complexity. Values are reported in Table 2.

Fig. 2: Mode selection. The input image is first partitioned into non-overlapping patches. For each patch, the M codecs are evaluated by their rate-distortion costs, and the codec minimizing this cost is selected, yielding the mode map m.

Fig. 3: Spatial encoding process. Given the mode map m, each image patch is encoded using the selected analysis transform and entropy model. Patches sharing the same mode are processed continuously. AE denotes arithmetic encoding.

Fig. 4: Visualization of mode maps for different numbers of modes. Mode maps are obtained with different numbers of competing modes (M ) at λ = 0.0002. Images are stefan-kunze-26931 and nomao-saeki-33553 from the CLIC 2020 dataset.
Fig. 5: BD-rate vs. encoding complexity on CLIC 2020. Following [15], encoding complexity for Cool-chic 4.0 is computed as 3× κdec× number of iterations.
Table 2: BD-rate vs. HEVC on CLIC 2020. Encoding and decoding complexity κenc and κdec are reported in kMAC/pixel.
